spec.reasoning: The task references specific cell addresses (A1) and column letters (B, C) in the instruction, so column reorder and blank_row_insert are unsafe (as proven by Attempt 3). Headers are also data values in this single-row layout, so column renames would require changing data too and risk breaking formula references in the instruction. I use sheet rename (Sheet1→WorkArea) as a lexical perturbation since the instruction never references the sheet name. I add a distractor column 'Notes' after column C to test whether the model is distracted by an extra column. No formulas should remain in the golden file — all existing formulas (B1 and C1) must be baked to their computed string values ('SL' and '-0035' respectively) before the model writes its answer to C1.
